title: Legislator
rationale: The role of a legislator involves significant human judgment, decision-making, and negotiation, which are less susceptible to automation by AI. However, AI can aid legislators by analyzing vast amounts of data for better decision-making and streamlining administrative tasks. Given Singapore's digital landscape and reliance on data-driven governance, AI will reshape some aspects of their work, but the need for human presence and judgment keeps the exposure moderate.

title: Senior statutory board official
rationale: Senior statutory board officials in Singapore primarily engage in policy formulation, strategic planning, and oversight, which require human judgment, leadership, and decision-making skills that are less susceptible to AI automation. However, AI tools can augment their work by enhancing data analysis and informing decision-making processes, thus having a moderate impact on the role.

title: Senior official of political party organisation
rationale: The role of a senior official in a political party organization involves significant strategic decision-making, negotiation, and relationship management, which are less susceptible to AI automation. While some aspects like data analysis or communications can benefit from AI tools, the need for human judgment, leadership, and political acumen remains crucial. In Singapore, where political landscapes and relationships are vital, AI is unlikely to significantly reshape such roles.

title: Senior official of employers’/workers’ and related economic-interest organisation
rationale: Senior officials in economic-interest organisations are involved in strategic decision-making, advocacy, and policy formulation, which involve complex human judgment and nuanced interpersonal communication. While AI can enhance productivity through data analysis and virtual communication tools, the core tasks require human insight into social and economic dynamics, thus limiting AI's direct applicability.

title: Senior official of humanitarian and other special-interest organisation
rationale: This occupation involves significant knowledge work, strategic decision-making, and leadership, which are areas where human judgment plays an essential role. While AI can aid in data analysis and communication, the nuanced understanding required for managing humanitarian and special-interest organizations limits full automation. Nevertheless, the highly digitized nature of Singapore's economy does increase exposure potential through enhanced productivity tools.

title: Chief operating officer/General manager
rationale: The role of a Chief Operating Officer or General Manager involves a significant amount of strategic planning, decision making, and human management, which AI can augment but not fully automate. While AI can assist with data analysis and streamline some administrative tasks, the need for human judgment, leadership, and oversight in complex business environments limits the overall exposure. In Singapore's highly digitised economy, managers may leverage AI to enhance productivity, but these roles remain predominantly reliant on human expertise.

title: Audit manager
rationale: Audit managers are likely to experience high AI exposure due to the digital nature of much of their work, which includes data analysis, risk assessment, and reporting. While AI can automate routine data processing, judgment and strategic decision-making remain reliant on human expertise. Therefore, although task automation will increase productivity, some human involvement will continue to be necessary.

title: Training manager
rationale: Training managers are involved in designing, overseeing, and implementing training programs, which involves significant human judgment and interpersonal skills. While AI can be used to analyze training effectiveness and assist in content delivery, the role requires adapting to organizational culture and employee needs, which are not easily replicable by AI. However, as training materials become more digital, certain aspects of the job may become more automated, leading to a moderate to high AI exposure.

title: Risk management manager
rationale: Risk management heavily relies on data analysis, scenario modeling, and evaluation of potential financial threats, all of which are tasks that AI can significantly enhance. While human judgment remains critical, particularly in decision-making and strategic planning, AI tools can automate and optimize much of the data-driven aspects of risk assessment.

title: Other business services/administration manager n.e.c.
rationale: This role largely involves strategic planning, decision-making, and oversight functions, which can be significantly enhanced by AI technologies analyzing data and optimizing processes. However, it also requires human judgment, interpersonal skills, and adaptability to dynamic environments, which are less susceptible to automation. Given Singapore's strong digital economy and service sector focus, these managers will leverage AI to boost productivity, yet the human element remains crucial.

title: Online sales channel manager
rationale: The role of an online sales channel manager is highly exposed to AI due to its digital nature, involving tasks such as analyzing online sales data, managing digital marketing campaigns, and optimizing online sales strategies. AI can automate data analysis, customer segmentation, and even digital ad placement, enhancing productivity and potentially reducing the need for human management. Singapore's advanced digital infrastructure amplifies these effects, making the occupation highly susceptible to AI reshaping.

title: Marketing manager
rationale: Marketing managers in Singapore are highly exposed to AI due to the predominantly digital nature of their work, such as data analysis, strategy development, and online campaign management. AI can automate many of these tasks, optimizing ad placements and customizing consumer interactions, thus reshaping how marketing strategies are formulated and executed. However, human judgment in creative strategy and client relationships still holds value.

title: Research and development manager
rationale: Research and development managers are primarily involved in knowledge work, overseeing projects that require critical thinking, data analysis, and strategic planning, which are areas of high AI exposure. AI can assist in data analysis and progression tracking, potentially reducing the need for as many human resources. However, the role also requires significant human judgment, creativity, and interpersonal skills which provide some resistance to complete AI disruption.

title: Call centre manager
rationale: The role of a call centre manager involves significant digital work, including data analysis, performance management, and communication, which can be enhanced by AI through automation and decision support systems. While human judgment and leadership in managing teams are required, AI can reshape how these tasks are performed, thereby increasing productivity and potentially reducing the need for managers.

title: Production manager in agriculture/fisheries
rationale: While production managers in agriculture and fisheries oversee primarily physical processes, aspects of their role such as data analysis, logistics management, and resource allocation can be digitized and optimized through AI. Automation in monitoring and reporting may increase productivity and efficiency, thereby reducing reliance on human-driven processes, but the necessity for in-person oversight and decision-making in unpredictable environments limits AI's impact to moderate levels.

title: Manufacturing manager
rationale: Manufacturing managers operate in a predominantly knowledge-based role involving planning, coordinating, and optimizing manufacturing processes. AI can significantly enhance productivity by automating planning and supply chain components and improving decision-making processes. However, human judgment and experience in managing teams and responding to dynamic production floor challenges remain essential, limiting full automation.

title: Construction manager
rationale: Construction managers primarily coordinate and supervise construction projects, requiring a mix of physical presence, human judgment, and digital planning. While AI can aid in project management tasks like scheduling and budgeting, many aspects such as on-site problem-solving and liaising with diverse stakeholders require human interaction and oversight. Therefore, AI exposure is moderate due to the significant integration of digital tools in planning and analysis.

title: Port/Shipping operations manager
rationale: The role of a Port/Shipping operations manager involves considerable knowledge work, requiring coordination and oversight of logistics, supply chain, and personnel. While AI can automate substantial parts of logistics management and data analysis, human judgment, negotiation, and real-time decision-making in unpredictable environments remain critical. Thus, while AI will increase productivity, the role still requires a human element for effective operation in Singapore's busy ports.

title: Other production/operations manager n.e.c.
rationale: As a manager in the ICT sector, this role involves overseeing digital and operational tasks, making AI exposure high. AI tools can optimize resource allocation, project management, and improve decision-making through data analytics. However, human judgment and leadership remain crucial, maintaining demand for managerial expertise.

title: Chief information officer/Chief technology officer/Chief information security
rationale: The role of a CIO/CTO/CISO is highly exposed to AI due to its focus on managing and securing digital infrastructure and leveraging technology for strategic purposes. While AI can enhance decision-making and streamline operations, the need for human judgment in strategy, leadership, and risk management remains. In Singapore's highly digitised economy, these roles will increasingly integrate AI for optimization, though not be entirely replaced.

title: Application development manager Alternative Title
rationale: Application development managers primarily oversee the development of software applications, a fundamentally digital work product. They manage teams, ensure project alignment with business strategies, and integrate AI-driven tools to enhance productivity and software capabilities. AI can automate many aspects of software development and management, increasing productivity and potentially reducing the need for manual oversight.

title: ICT product design manager
rationale: As an ICT product design manager, the role involves significant digital knowledge, communication, and management tasks. AI can aid in design processes, data analysis, and project management, enhancing productivity and decision-making. While human judgment and leadership are crucial, the digital nature of the work increases AI exposure.

title: Preschool education/Early intervention centre manager
rationale: This role involves predominantly knowledge work, including administrative management, curriculum planning, and staff coordination, which can be partially digitized. While AI can assist in operational efficiencies and data management, significant aspects require human judgment and interpersonal skills, especially given the context of managing educational and developmental services for young children.

title: Health services manager
rationale: Health services managers primarily engage in knowledge-intensive tasks such as strategic planning, operational management, and coordination of care services, which can be enhanced by AI through data analysis and decision support systems. However, the need for human judgment, communication, and regulatory compliance in healthcare settings poses barriers to full automation, keeping AI exposure high but not at its maximum.

title: Aged care services manager
rationale: Aged care services managers have a high level of digital and knowledge work, involving the coordination, planning, and management of care services, which can be augmented by AI systems for better efficiency and decision-making. However, the role still requires significant human interaction, empathy, and judgment to manage staff and address the complex and personalized needs of elderly clients, limiting full AI automation.

title: Volunteer manager
rationale: The role of a volunteer manager involves significant human interaction, judgment, and management skills that are less likely to be fully automated by AI. While digital tools can assist with scheduling, communication, and data management tasks, the core duties require understanding human motivation and relationship management, which are complex and not entirely digitizable. However, AI can increase productivity by automating administrative tasks.

title: Education/Training institution manager n.e.c.
rationale: This occupation involves both administrative tasks and strategic decision-making which are primarily knowledge-based, therefore susceptible to AI tools enhancing productivity. However, the role also necessitates significant human interaction, leadership, and judgment in managing staff and responding to educational needs, which are less easily automated.

title: Financial risk manager
rationale: Financial risk management is highly exposed to AI due to its reliance on data analysis, modeling, and forecasting, tasks that can be significantly enhanced by AI's advanced analytics capabilities. However, human judgment is still crucial in interpreting results and making strategic decisions, moderating AI's impact to some extent.

title: Other professional/financial/community/social services manager n.e.c.
rationale: Managers in professional, financial, and community services oversee operations that may include significant digital tasks such as communication, analysis, and planning which AI can augment. However, their roles still require real-time decision-making, interpersonal skills, and leadership in unpredictable environments, which are less easily automated by AI. Hence, AI exposure is high but not extreme.

title: Hotel operations/Lodging services manager
rationale: Hotel operations and lodging services managers are significantly exposed to AI as many functions like reservations, inventory management, and customer interactions can be optimized with digital solutions. However, the role requires a high level of human interaction, decision-making, and the ability to manage dynamic environments, which cannot be entirely replaced by AI. In Singapore's digitised economy, the trend towards AI-powered service enhancements will reshape certain managerial tasks but not fully automate them.

title: Food and beverage operations manager
rationale: Food and beverage operations managers oversee both digital and physical aspects of their operations, with responsibilities including inventory management, scheduling, and customer service. While AI can assist with data analytics, inventory optimization, and customer relationship management, the role requires significant human judgment, decision-making, and interpersonal skills to handle staff and customer interactions effectively.

title: Wholesale trade manager
rationale: Wholesale trade managers largely deal with supply chain logistics, analyzing market trends, and managing inventory—all tasks that can see significant digital augmentation through AI. AI can optimize supply chains, forecast market needs, and automate administrative processes, thus reshaping the occupation by reducing the need for manual data handling and decision-making. However, human judgment in negotiations and strategy remains crucial.

title: Sports centre manager
rationale: As a sports centre manager, the role involves some digital tasks, such as scheduling, budgeting, and communication, which can be enhanced by AI. However, it also requires significant human judgment and interaction, directly managing staff and ensuring customer satisfaction, which are less susceptible to automation. The blend of managerial duties and on-site oversight places the AI exposure in a moderate range.

title: Recreation centre manager n.e.c.
rationale: Recreation centre managers deal with both physical and digital tasks, such as overseeing facility operations, staff management, and customer service. AI can enhance efficiency in scheduling, resource allocation, and visitor management through digital platforms. However, the role still requires significant human judgment and interaction, such as handling customer relations and ensuring a high-quality service environment, limiting full AI automation.

title: Arts/Cultural centre manager
rationale: The role of an arts/cultural centre manager involves significant digital and knowledge work, including strategic planning, financial management, and marketing which can be enhanced by AI tools. However, it also requires nuanced human judgment and interaction, such as engaging with artists and the community, which are less susceptible to automation. Given Singapore's focus on digital transformation, AI exposure is moderately high.

title: Other services manager n.e.c.
rationale: Other services managers n.e.c. in Singapore are likely to have high AI exposure due to the city's digitized economy and the managerial roles that involve significant amounts of decision-making, strategic planning, and data analysis. While these tasks can be augmented by AI, the human judgment and leadership aspects require skills beyond automation. Therefore, while AI can enhance productivity, it won't entirely replace human roles.

title: Physicist/Astronomer
rationale: The work of physicists and astronomers is highly knowledge-based and involves significant data analysis and modeling, which AI can substantially augment. AI's capability in processing vast datasets and performing simulations can lead to increased productivity and efficiency in research. However, the requirement for innovative thinking, hypothesis generation, and complex problem-solving means complete automation is unlikely, preserving some human roles.

title: Meteorologist
rationale: Meteorologists work primarily with digital data for weather forecasting, which is a field that can be significantly enhanced by AI through data analysis, pattern recognition, and predictive modeling. The reliance on computational models and large data sets means that AI can dramatically increase productivity and accuracy, requiring fewer human meteorologists to perform these tasks. However, the need for human judgment in unusual weather events or public communication still necessitates some human involvement.

title: Chemist
rationale: Chemists primarily engage in knowledge work involving research, analysis, and data interpretation, much of which can be digitized and enhanced by AI. AI can streamline tasks like modeling chemical reactions, analyzing data, and even suggesting new compounds, increasing productivity. However, experimental validation, which requires laboratory work, remains partially resistant to full automation.

title: Geologist/Geophysicist and related physical science professional n.e.c.
rationale: Geologists and geophysicists perform significant knowledge work in analyzing and interpreting geological data, which AI can substantially assist with through advanced data modeling and simulations. However, fieldwork and site-specific investigations remain integral to the role, offering moderate protection from full automation. In Singapore's context, the integration with digital tools is high due to the tech-savvy environment, increasing AI exposure compared to more hands-on geoscientific work.

title: Actuary
rationale: Actuaries primarily engage in statistical analysis, modeling, and data interpretation, which are highly digital and algorithm-driven tasks that AI can enhance or automate. Given Singapore's advanced financial services sector and focus on data analytics, actuaries are highly exposed to AI-driven tools that can perform risk assessments, forecasting, and predictive modeling more efficiently. However, AI might not fully replace the nuanced judgment and decision-making skills of experienced actuaries, but it will reshape the occupation significantly.

title: Data scientist
rationale: Data scientists' work is primarily digital, involving data analysis, modeling, and communication of insights. AI has the potential to automate many tasks in this field, such as data preprocessing, basic model building, and pattern recognition. However, human input is still crucial for designing new models, understanding complex business contexts, and ethical considerations. Given Singapore's digitized economy, the exposure is heightened.

title: Other statistical/mathematical/actuarial professional n.e.c.
rationale: Statistical, mathematical, and actuarial professionals are heavily involved in data analysis, predictions, and modeling, which are activities easily enhanced by AI. AI can automate complex calculations and data processing tasks, improving efficiency but also potentially reducing the need for human involvement. However, they still require domain-specific expertise and human judgment to interpret AI-generated outputs and make contextually informed decisions.

title: Biologist/Botanist/Zoologist and related professional n.e.c.
rationale: While these professions involve significant knowledge work, which can be augmented by AI (e.g., data analysis, simulation models), the work often also requires fieldwork and real-world experiments. The combination of digital data processing and direct observation in nature balances the AI exposure to a moderate level. In Singapore, the advanced digital infrastructure may increase the digital aspect of their work, thus moderate exposure.

title: Farming/Forestry/Fishery adviser n.e.c.
rationale: While the advisory role involves digital elements like data analysis and report writing, it requires considerable human judgment, environmental assessment, and interaction with clients in the field. However, AI can aid in data collection and analysis, enhancing decision-making and reducing the need for routine advisory tasks. Given Singapore's focus on technology and sustainability, AI adoption is likely, but the need for contextual human expertise persists.

title: Environmental protection and related professional n.e.c.
rationale: Environmental protection professionals engage in a mix of knowledge-based analysis and fieldwork. Tasks such as data analysis, environmental modeling, and report writing are highly susceptible to AI automation, particularly in Singapore's digital economy. However, field assessments, regulatory compliance checks, and stakeholder consultations require human judgment and presence, limiting the overall exposure.

title: Pharmacologist and related professional n.e.c.
rationale: Pharmacologists primarily work with digital data and software for drug discovery, development, and analysis, exposing them considerably to AI's capabilities in data analysis and predictive modeling. While expert human judgment is essential, AI tools can accelerate research and improve precision, altering the demand for human researchers in digital environments. Given Singapore's focus on digitisation and advanced scientific industries, AI will substantially influence pharmacologists.

title: Process engineer
rationale: Process engineers engage in a mix of digital and physical tasks, such as designing systems and processes for manufacturing, which can be highly enhanced by AI. While their work involves significant technology and knowledge work, human oversight and adaptation to specific physical conditions remain essential. In Singapore's digitised economy, AI can boost productivity in process analysis, simulation, and optimization.

title: Building construction engineer
rationale: Building construction engineers perform a mix of physical oversight and digital tasks, such as designing and analyzing construction plans with specialized software. While AI can enhance design and planning processes, on-site project management and human judgment in dynamic environments limit transformation by AI. Singapore's digitized economy may see greater tech integration but hands-on project needs remain essential.

title: Environmental engineer
rationale: Environmental engineers primarily engage in knowledge work that involves analysis, design, and project management, which are areas increasingly supported by AI through simulations, data modeling, and predictive analytics. However, this occupation also requires on-site assessments, compliance with environmental regulations, and human oversight, which act as constraints to AI's full automation of the role.

title: Port equipment engineer
rationale: Port equipment engineers perform a mix of physical and knowledge work, as their role involves hands-on management and maintenance of port machinery as well as analyzing performance data and developing improvement strategies. While AI can assist in diagnostics and data analysis, the need for on-site presence and manual intervention keeps the AI exposure at a moderate level.

title: Food/Drink technologist
rationale: Food/Drink technologists are involved in the development, processing, and quality control of food and beverages. While many aspects of this role rely on applying scientific knowledge, conducting experiments, and problem-solving, AI can significantly assist in data analysis, formulation processes, and product testing. However, the need for hands-on experimentation and sensory evaluation limits complete automation, thus leading to moderate to high AI exposure.

title: Mining engineer/Metallurgist and related professional
rationale: Mining engineers and metallurgists engage in both knowledge-intensive tasks such as analysis and planning of mining operations and processing, and physical tasks including site inspections and evaluations. While AI can optimize planning and analysis functions, the need for on-site presence in often unpredictable environments limits complete automation. The role involves a blend of digital and physical elements, placing AI exposure at a moderate level.

title: Rolling stock engineer
rationale: Rolling stock engineers involve both physical tasks and complex problem-solving, designing, or maintaining railway-related equipment. While some digital modeling and diagnostic tasks can be automated, the role requires on-site assessments, real-world testing, and manual interventions. The Singapore context, with its focus on efficient public transportation, may leverage AI for predictive maintenance and simulations but will still require human oversight and decision-making.

title: Other engineering professional n.e.c.
rationale: Engineering professionals not elsewhere classified typically engage in specialized knowledge work that involves both physical and digital components. While AI can assist with simulations, modeling, and data analysis, these roles often require creativity, problem-solving, and an understanding of physical systems that AI cannot fully replicate. In Singapore's highly digitized economy, engineering processes may incorporate AI substantially, but human oversight remains critical.

title: Lift engineer
rationale: Lift engineers involve both physical and technical tasks, including maintenance, inspection, and repair of elevator systems, which require on-site manual interventions and problem-solving. While AI can aid in predictive maintenance through data analysis and monitoring, the hands-on nature of the work limits full automation, resulting in a relatively low exposure to AI.

title: Embedded systems engineer
rationale: Embedded systems engineers work primarily with digital systems, designing and coding software that interacts with hardware. As much of their work product is digital, involving tasks like simulation, testing, and analysis, AI can significantly affect their productivity by automating routine coding and testing processes. However, complete automation is unlikely due to the need for human judgment in understanding complex hardware-software interactions.

title: Telecommunications engineer
rationale: Telecommunications engineers primarily engage in designing, implementing, and maintaining digital communication systems, which involves significant digital knowledge work. AI can automate many design and network optimization tasks and enhance predictive maintenance through data analysis. However, the need for physical oversight and bespoke decision-making in complex situations slightly reduces exposure compared to purely digital roles.

title: Building architect
rationale: Building architects perform a mix of tasks that involve digital design, complex project planning, and onsite assessments. While AI can significantly enhance design automation and data analysis processes, the creative and situational problem-solving aspects, along with real-world site evaluations, still require human input. The digital aspect of architectural design suggests moderately high AI exposure.

title: Landscape designer
rationale: Landscape design involves a mix of creative design work, requiring significant human judgment, and knowledge of outdoor environments. While the design process can be digitized, with AI tools potentially aiding in visual rendering and environmental analytics, the need for on-site assessments and client interactions provides a buffer against full AI automation. Consequently, the occupation exhibits high but not maximum exposure to AI.

title: Product/Industrial designer
rationale: As a product/industrial designer, much of the work involves digital design tools and software, making the role highly exposed to AI. In Singapore's digitized economy, AI can automate repetitive design tasks and provide advanced analytics capabilities, thus reshaping creativity and decision-making processes. However, human creativity and the ability to consider user experience nuances provide some buffer against complete automation.

title: Urban and related planner n.e.c.
rationale: Urban and related planners n.e.c. conduct significant knowledge work involving data analysis, GIS, and digital modeling, which can be reshaped by AI through enhanced data processing and predictive analytics. However, the need for human judgment and context-specific decision-making in urban planning keeps the AI exposure moderate to high, as planners must also engage with stakeholders and consider complex social and environmental factors.

title: Surveyor/Cartographer n.e.c.
rationale: Surveyors and cartographers deal with both digital and physical aspects of geospatial data. While AI and technology can automate the processing and analysis of digital maps and spatial data, on-site data collection and validation require human intervention. The profession involves a mix of knowledge work, such as geographic information systems (GIS), and physical tasks, which limits full automation but allows for significant AI integration in digital aspects.

title: Graphic/Multimedia designer/artist n.e.c.
rationale: Graphic and multimedia designers primarily create digital content and leverage software tools, making them highly exposed to AI. AI can automate design iterations, generate creative elements, and assist in multimedia editing and animations, thus reshaping the field significantly. However, while AI can aid routine tasks, human creativity and artistic judgment still play a critical role in novel design concepts.

title: Service designer
rationale: Service designers predominantly engage in knowledge work, creating and optimizing service systems which heavily relies on digital tools for research, prototyping, and communication. AI can significantly enhance their productivity by automating tasks such as data analysis, trend forecasting, and even generating initial design concepts, hence increasing exposure. In Singapore's digitized economy, the demand for improved and innovative service solutions further amplifies AI's impact on this role.

title: Marine superintendent
rationale: Marine superintendents oversee the safe and efficient operation of ships, which involves a mix of physical presence for ship inspections and digital tasks like logistics coordination, compliance checks, and reporting. While AI can enhance efficiency in monitoring ship systems and automating reporting, the physical and decision-making aspects of the role limit AI exposure. In the context of Singapore's advanced maritime industry, digital tools are prevalent, yet the human judgment necessary for real-time problem-solving on ships moderates AI impact.

title: Aircraft pilot and related professional n.e.c.
rationale: Pilots perform critical knowledge-intensive tasks such as flight planning, navigation, and communication, which are increasingly supported by AI and automation. However, the need for real-time human judgment and presence for safety-critical decisions in unpredictable environments limits AI's ability to fully replace them. In Singapore's advanced aviation sector, AI may enhance productivity and safety but not completely substitute the human role.

title: General practitioner/physician
rationale: General practitioners perform a mix of physical examinations, human interaction, and decision-making that involves complex judgment. While AI can assist with diagnostic tools and administrative tasks, human interaction and customized patient care require skills that AI cannot easily replicate. However, AI can significantly enhance productivity by streamlining information processing and supporting diagnostic and treatment recommendations.

title: Psychiatrist
rationale: While aspects of a psychiatrist's role, such as diagnostic analysis and treatment planning, can be supported by AI, the occupation heavily relies on real-time human interaction, empathy, and understanding of non-verbal cues. In Singapore's context, where healthcare is advanced, AI tools may augment psychiatrists' efficiency but not replace the core human-centered elements of their work.

title: Specialist medical practitioner (medical) n.e.c.
rationale: Specialist medical practitioners perform complex, hands-on surgical tasks that require high levels of human dexterity, decision-making, and real-time problem-solving. While AI can assist in diagnostics and planning, the actual surgical procedures and bedside interactions heavily rely on human skills which are not easily automated by AI.

title: Specialist medical practitioner (surgical) n.e.c.
rationale: While AI can assist surgical specialists through enhanced diagnostic tools and robotic surgery support, the core tasks require significant human judgment, dexterity, and experience in unpredictable environments. Given the high-stakes nature of surgery and the critical need for real-time decision-making, AI's role is likely to be supportive rather than substitutive, maintaining a low to moderate exposure level.

title: Specialist medical practitioner (other specialisation) n.e.c.
rationale: While medical professionals rely on technology for diagnosis and treatment, human judgment, manual skills, and patient interaction play crucial roles that AI can't fully replicate. In Singapore's tech-driven economy, AI may assist in diagnostics and administrative duties, but the core medical tasks require human expertise and decision-making.

title: Registered nurse and related nursing professional (excluding enrolled nurse)
rationale: Registered nurses have a moderate level of AI exposure as their work involves direct patient care, requiring physical presence and human interaction. While AI can assist in diagnostics, monitoring, and administrative tasks, the essential hands-on and empathetic components of nursing care remain largely unaffected by automation. In Singapore's highly digitised healthcare environment, AI will augment but not replace nursing roles.

title: Traditional Chinese medicine acupuncturist
rationale: Traditional Chinese medicine acupuncturists rely heavily on hands-on physical skills and real-time human interaction, which are challenging for AI to replicate. The role requires intricate knowledge of body systems and personal patient care, limiting the potential for AI automation. While some digital scheduling and patient record tasks might be susceptible to AI, the core therapeutic engagement remains largely unaffected.

title: Veterinarian
rationale: Veterinarians perform a mix of physical tasks and diagnostic knowledge work, examining and treating animals which require real-time human interaction and judgment. While AI can assist in areas like diagnosis and data analysis, the hands-on nature of surgeries and treatments, as well as the need for empathy and communication with pet owners, limits AI's direct impact.

title: Specialised dentist
rationale: Specialised dentists perform a range of physical procedures and require fine motor skills, manual dexterity, and patient interaction, which are challenging for AI to replicate. Although AI can assist with diagnostics and planning, the core of their work requires hands-on clinical skills and decision-making in real-time with patients.

title: Pharmacist n.e.c.
rationale: Pharmacists engage in a significant amount of digital work, such as medication management, prescription processing, and data analysis. However, their need for direct patient interaction, judgment in patient care, and adherence to complex regulations limit full automation potential. Their work in providing advice and ensuring safety also involves a critical human element.

title: Environmental and occupational health and hygiene professional n.e.c.
rationale: Environmental and occupational health and hygiene professionals engage in a mix of physical site assessments and digital data analysis. While AI can automate data interpretation and predictive modelling, the assessment of site-specific conditions and compliance with regulations requires human judgment. Additionally, the role involves direct consultation with stakeholders and adapting to complex, unpredictable environments.

title: Physiotherapist
rationale: Physiotherapy involves a significant amount of hands-on, physical interaction with patients, which limits AI's direct impact. While AI can enhance diagnostics, treatment planning, and administrative tasks, the actual therapeutic interventions and human judgment required in unpredictable environments remain beyond AI's reach. In Singapore's context, technology may complement but not replace physiotherapists' core duties.

title: Nutritionist
rationale: While nutritionists utilize digital tools for data analytics and to track client progress, their role involves significant human interaction, personalized consultations, and real-time decision-making that AI cannot fully replicate. In Singapore's service-oriented economy, the demand for personalized health and nutrition advice will remain strong, moderating AI's impact.

title: Speech therapist
rationale: Speech therapy involves significant real-time human interaction, requiring professional judgment to adapt treatment plans to individual patient needs. While AI can assist with diagnostic procedures and tracking progress, the therapeutic process remains largely manual and face-to-face. There is moderate exposure due to the possibility of AI tools augmenting parts of the work, such as data collection and preliminary assessments.

title: Optometrist
rationale: Optometrists perform a mix of physical and knowledge-based tasks, including patient examinations and interpreting digital scans of eye health. While AI can assist in diagnostics with image recognition and analysis, the need for physical presence and human interaction in assessing patient conditions limits AI's impact. The judgment required in customizing patient care plans and prescriptions ensures some resistance to full automation.

title: Occupational therapist
rationale: While some aspects of occupational therapy may benefit from AI-driven tools for diagnostics and treatment planning, the profession largely requires hands-on interaction with patients to develop personalized rehabilitation programs. The necessity for direct human contact, assessment of physical environments, and tailoring therapy to individual needs in real-time provides significant resistance to full automation by AI.

title: Other health professional n.e.c.
rationale: Health professionals not elsewhere classified (n.e.c.) would include various specialized roles that often require significant human interaction, physical presence, and application of specialized judgment in unpredictable environments. While AI can assist in diagnostics and administrative tasks, the core of these roles involves personalized patient care which AI cannot currently replicate. However, AI tools can enhance productivity, thereby influencing the overall exposure assessment.

title: University/Polytechnic/Higher education teacher n.e.c.
rationale: Higher education teaching involves significant digital elements such as online course management, digital resources, and virtual lectures, which makes it susceptible to AI tools that can automate administrative tasks and offer personalized learning experiences. However, the necessity for human judgment, mentoring, and in-person engagement ensures that some aspects remain less exposed to AI.

title: Junior college/Pre-university teacher
rationale: Junior college/pre-university teachers primarily engage in knowledge work, including preparing lessons, grading, and academic counseling. AI can aid with tasks like grading and data analysis but human interaction for classroom teaching and personalized student guidance remains essential. The digital nature of lesson planning and resource creation in Singapore's educational context increases their AI exposure.

title: Technical/Vocational/Commercial education institute teacher/trainer
rationale: This role involves both knowledge work and significant interpersonal interaction. While the delivery and analysis of educational content could potentially be enhanced by AI, the need for human judgment, mentorship, and real-time engagement with students limits full automation. Singapore's emphasis on a highly digitised education system may increase AI integration, but the occupation remains primarily human-interactive.

title: Secondary school teacher
rationale: Secondary school teachers' work includes both digital and physical components, such as delivering lectures, grading, and tailoring educational content to student needs. While AI can assist with grading and providing supplementary digital resources, the core teaching function requires real-time interaction, adaptation, and social skills that limit AI's complete substitution in Singapore's highly digitized education sector.

title: Primary school teacher
rationale: Primary school teachers perform predominantly knowledge-based tasks, but their work requires significant human interaction, emotional intelligence, and hands-on involvement with students, which limits full AI automation. AI can assist with lesson planning, administrative tasks, and provide personalized learning experiences, enhancing productivity but not replacing the essential human elements in teaching.

title: Education methods specialist n.e.c.
rationale: While the role involves the development of educational methods, which is predominantly knowledge work, it also requires significant human judgment and understanding of learning processes. AI can support content delivery and personalized learning plans, but creative and empathetic elements in education methods design are less amenable to automation. Singapore's digitized economy enhances the AI exposure, especially for the analytical aspect of the job.

title: Special education teacher n.e.c.
rationale: Special education teachers require significant human interaction, real-time problem-solving, and emotional intelligence to cater to diverse student needs. While AI can assist with creating tailored educational plans and digital resources, the hands-on, adaptive, and empathetic nature of teaching students with special needs limits AI's direct applicability. Consequently, AI exposure is moderate due to the essential human elements involved.

title: Teaching professional n.e.c.
rationale: Teaching professionals not elsewhere classified (n.e.c.) are involved in knowledge work that requires human judgment and adaptability, such as understanding student needs and adjusting pedagogical approaches. While AI can assist in developing learning materials and providing data-driven insights, the in-person and interactive elements of teaching limit full automation. Singapore's digital infrastructure can enhance productivity, but high-touch educational roles remain relatively protected.

title: Tax accountant
rationale: Tax accounting is predominantly a digital occupation involving data analysis, computation, and the application of tax laws, all of which can be highly automated by AI. While professional judgment and personalized client interactions still require human input, AI systems can significantly streamline compliance tasks, calculations, and regulatory updates, reducing the need for human intervention.

title: Business valuer
rationale: Business valuers primarily conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis to assess the value of a company. This work can be heavily supported by AI, which excels at processing large datasets, identifying trends, and executing financial modeling. Given Singapore's emphasis on its digital economy, AI tools can significantly streamline and enhance business valuation processes, reducing the need for human intervention.

title: Financial analyst and related professional n.e.c.
rationale: Financial analysts operate in a highly digital environment, analyzing large datasets and generating insights through software, which AI can significantly enhance or automate. AI tools can process vast amounts of financial data to provide predictive analytics and investment recommendations, reducing the need for human analysis. Singapore's position as a global financial hub means that its financial professionals are likely to be among the first to adopt AI-driven solutions, increasing their exposure.

title: Securities/Finance dealer n.e.c.
rationale: Securities/finance dealers work in highly digital environments with tasks like trading, analyzing market data, and financial modeling, which are well-suited for AI. AI can enhance trading efficiency and productivity through algorithms that analyze large datasets faster and more accurately than humans. In Singapore's advanced finance sector, AI is likely to significantly impact job structures and demand.

title: Securities/Finance broker n.e.c.
rationale: Securities and finance brokers engage heavily in digital activities like market analysis, data interpretation, and transaction execution, making their work highly susceptible to AI automation. AI's capabilities to process large volumes of data and predict market trends can significantly enhance productivity, reducing the demand for human brokers. However, elements of human judgment and relationship management may still require some human presence.

title: Insurance underwriter
rationale: Insurance underwriting involves assessing risk and determining insurance policy premiums, which is heavily data-driven and involves digital processes. AI systems can automate data collection, risk assessment, and even initial decision-making, thus reshaping the role significantly. The need for nuanced human judgment in atypical or complex cases remains, but the core tasks are highly susceptible to AI automation.

title: Sustainability project development/management professional
rationale: Sustainability project management involves substantial digital work such as data analysis, project planning, and policy development, which AI can greatly enhance through automation and predictive analytics. While the role requires some human judgment and stakeholder engagement, much of the strategic and administrative tasks are prone to AI reshaping, especially in a digital-forward economy like Singapore.

title: Risk analyst (non-financial)

Given Singapore's digitised economy and the nature of this role, AI tools can significantly automate data gathering, analysis, and risk prediction tasks. However, strategic elements involving human judgment and decision-making may still require human oversight, though at a reduced scale.

rationale: Industrial relations officers engage in work that involves a mix of human judgment and communication, as they manage employee relations and negotiations. While some tasks can be supported by AI, such as data analysis and document preparation, the role requires a significant amount of interpersonal skills and conflict resolution, which are less susceptible to automation. In Singapore's digitally advanced economy, productivity tools may enhance their efficiency but will not entirely replace the essential human element of their work.

title: Training/Staff development professional
rationale: Training and staff development professionals deal mostly with knowledge work that involves designing training modules, analyzing workforce needs, and delivering educational content. AI has the potential to automate and enhance these tasks by personalizing learning experiences and generating training content, although human judgment and interpersonal skills remain essential for understanding needs and motivations. Singapore's digitized economy suggests a higher AI exposure due to the integration of AI technologies in digital education platforms.

title: Intellectual property examiner
rationale: The role of an intellectual property examiner involves analyzing digital documents, assessing legal compliance, and ensuring protection of intellectual property. These tasks are highly knowledge-based and involve digital information processing that AI can significantly enhance through automation and advanced data analysis. title: Other administration professional n.e.c.

title: Visual artist n.e.c.
